data_limitations: Many records from fee_award_decision and attorney_affidavit sources contain aggregate hour totals rather than full daily timesheet entries. Stage-level breakdowns are often inferred from case descriptions.
effective_nodes is a proxy for case complexity (motions, witnesses, documents, parties) and is estimated when not explicitly stated in source documents.
Billing_survey records represent typical case hours estimated from state-average hourly rates, not actual billed timesheets.
Court_fee_schedule records use guideline rates and representative case complexity assumptions.
Academic_dataset records (Michigan RAND study) use mean hours from time-study observations, not individual attorney timesheets.
Some high-value records (e.g., Enron, FTX) have hours estimated from total fees and blended rates because detailed hour breakdowns were not available in search results.
Hong Kong data could not be retrieved due to search service errors; UK SCCO and CAT data used as Commonwealth proxy.
No record is a simple fee_schedule_range converted to timesheet. All records include explicit hour estimates with source attribution.
verification_notes: All records include source_url linking to verified court decisions, affidavits, academic reports, or survey publications. Records marked 'true' in verified column have been cross-checked against source documents.
